summary: This notebook demonstrates a production-ready inference pipeline that combines three distinct deep learning architectures trained on multi-modal sensor data (IMU, TOF, and Thermal). It emphasizes robust temporal sequence handling by padding to the 95th percentile, applying gravity-invariant acceleration features, and extracting region-based statistics from depth sensors. The core contribution is a weighted horizontal blending strategy that optimally merges predictions from CNN-LSTM/GRU, CNN-BERT, and Gated-GRU models to maximize leaderboard performance. Readers should learn how to structure a reproducible ensemble workflow, handle quaternion-based sensor physics, and fine-tune blend weights for time-series gesture recognition.
competition_flow: Loads pre-trained TensorFlow and PyTorch models from Kaggle datasets, processes raw IMU/TOF/Thermal sequences with physics-based feature engineering and scaling, generates predictions via model averaging, and applies optimized weighted blending for final submission.
data_reading: Reads sequence data via polars and pandas, loading precomputed artifacts (feature columns, scalers, sequence lengths, gesture classes) from Kaggle datasets using np.load and joblib.load.
data_processing: Fills missing values with forward/backward fill and zeros, pads sequences to a fixed length (95th percentile), scales features with StandardScaler, replaces invalid TOF values (-1) with NaN, and defines MixupGenerator for training augmentation.
features_engineering: Gravity-invariant linear acceleration (via quaternion rotation), linear acceleration magnitude & jerk, angular velocity & angular distance from quaternions, TOF region statistics (mean, std, min, max across 2/4/8/16/32 regions), and magnitude/jerk derivatives for IMU.
models: Two-branch CNN with SE blocks + BiLSTM + BiGRU + Attention (TensorFlow)
Three-branch CNN with SE blocks + BERT encoder + Classifier (PyTorch)
cv_strategy: StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5) and 10-fold splits (loaded from pre-trained artifacts)
ensembling: Blends predictions from three external models using optimized weights (e.g., A:0.53, B:0.18, C:0.29) and applies a [70 x 30] directed random scheme for post-processing, using median averaging for Model 1 and mean averaging for Model 2.
insights: Quaternion-based gravity removal is critical for accurate IMU acceleration features.
TOF depth data benefits from both raw pixel statistics and multi-resolution region aggregations.
Combining CNN spatial extraction with BERT/GRU temporal modeling captures complex gesture dynamics effectively.
Weighted horizontal blending of diverse architectures consistently outperforms single-model baselines.
